I want you to know that it relates to your ability to either expand the service that's working or to contract a service in order to get it to expand long term. I'm going to explain both of those based on the 22 Immutable Laws of Branding. The second perspective I'm going to give you on these two words is based on how products or services are selling or how you're able to deliver on products and services that you're selling to people or have sold is going to be based on your individual personality type and your company's personality type to whether it's an expansive mindset or a contracted mindset and I'll also explain how that works from a psychological perspective of your organization. That being said, let's first talk about how it relates to products and services with your ability to expand a service or to contract a service with the intention to expand. Let me do explain. There's a book called The 22 Immutable Laws of Branding. Don't get it misconstrued with the 22 Immutable Laws of Marketing. That was the 22 Immutable Laws of Branding. In that book, the first law and the second law are the law of expansion and the law of contraction. The law of expansion talks about American Express. Talks about American Express at one point owning 27% of the entire credit card market share. Now out of 27%, that was because they were a very prestigious brand. They catered to a particular niche. They only worked with executives. They only had a certain type of card. They had a very serious list of qualifications and that was it. That's what they were known for. They then chose to expand. Now the law of expansion talks about your sales long term actually contract and your brand loses brand equity and value. So let me do explain. American Express got this CEO. His name was Tom Suntner. Tom came in and he was like, we're going to come out with 13 new types of credit cards every year. Tom, what are you doing? The next year he came out 13. The next year he came out 13. Now all of a sudden hundreds of thousands, if not tens of millions of people at a certain point are signing up for American Express that were never doing it before. You're probably thinking in your mind, wow, that's incredible. That sounds awesome Jeremy. Why is this a negative thing? Let me do explain further. Short term gains, he got a lot of revenue, right? Fantastic. Long term, he fucked himself and his organization. American Express now only owns 14% of the entire market share of credit cards. Why? Because they diluted their brand and from diluting their brand they diluted customer trust and by diluting customer trust they had no longer the ability to retain somebody long term. Their attrition rate went through the group, meaning they lost customers in a high frequency in a short period of time and all of a sudden American Express instead of expanding contracted. Let's go on the other side of the fence. Let's go to the contraction perspective. Now if you like Subway for example, Subway or Starbucks is another great one. They master one thing and that one thing for Subway for example is making a footlong or making a six inch sub. They also sell chips, they sell soda but their one thing is hoes. It's that little sub, right? Cool. Check this out. Because they chose to contract a model and normally it's used to expand, they were able to expand massively and create a national chain, a national franchise chain of debt that crushes a revenue every year and has a massive impact across the entire world now. They're an international franchise. Subway I'm not going to make up some percentages for the market share that they own versus the market share they know now but let's compare them to the national or I'm sorry, the non-existent national deli chain that doesn't exist near you. How many different local delis keep you in name within your city? Well in my city and here in Miami I can go up to Aventura and I know that there's about 10 different Jewish delis that I can go into plus there's whatever other delis are in that specific area. I know that in this very area low to my island there's two different delis right down the road. I know there's another three or four on Biscayne and then I know that there's however many more there are all throughout Miami scattered but none of them are national. All of them are local probably at one or two locations at max. They all sell the exact same shit. What is the exact same shit you asked Jeremy? Great question. That's them having a deli right where you can get meats, cheeses, peppers, all the other stuff that Subway has for example and you can get the chips and the hoes and all that other stuff but they sell 10 different types of sandwiches and they sell melted stuff and they might have like gyro and they might have how many different things all within those things right because they have all that scattered attention within their organization they can never expand past that one location. Why? Why does Subway contract its services and get massive expansion comparatively taking the exact same concept of the deli but only offering one thing? Is it the power of choice? No. It's a log expansion versus log contraction. If you want more details on these 22 immutable laws of branding we'd be happy to describe them for more. For our sake and for time sake and for your ability to understand this as a digital agency on our lens related to something where I can story tell for you. I in my business once again as a digital agency on there the reason that I switched to personality brand development exclusively that I offer the exact same services I am literally like the national deli chain for Subway for example. I take the deli which is all the digital services that I just told you I offer and I now only offer them for personality brand development. You should take all the digital agency services that I'm telling you and only offer them for real estate people or only offer them for dentists or only offer them for this because you become a specialist and your ability to expand becomes compounding and projected through time meaning as soon as you continue to become an expert you gain speed you gain momentum you gain the ability to become greater. For example my videographer he currently goes and he shoots at live and story and all these different clubs but he also shoots me but he also shoots models but he also shoots all these other people at different times and different places and different scenes with different intentions right. He'll never be known for one particular thing such as the ability to come in set up an environment to shoot content like this get a percentage of the person's business when he's shooting a course form and get a flat fee charge just for the time that he has invested for showing up or for example if he only shot at clubs he could be known as an expert photographer, videographer, content creator all within that one organization and that one niche. His ability to compound his results through time would be substantial if he focused on one thing comparatively to scattering the attention across everything just for short term games which most of us do. Most of us take the short term revenue of all the different opportunities that are around us all the time instead of only looking for the opportunities within one niche or one area where there's always the same amount of opportunities but it takes that one little week or that one month of transitional focus and realignment and calibration in order to benefit from that kind of result. Most of us don't have the time to put away to do that. That being said the law of contraction would tell you if you can track what you do you technically expand massively in that area on which you've contracted a service. So law number two I personally did this with contracting everything that I offer to only narrowing it to personality brand development. What happened that same month? Great question. Tai Lopez hired me, Andy Vercilla hired me, three other large personality brands hired me including the guy Keith mentioned called her mother, Manjulunga with a TV show and I generated over a hundred thousand dollars a month more comparatively to what I generated my entire business with writing a digital agency servicing anybody and everybody who came in. Narrow your digital agency to one niche, contract what it is you do for your products and services, offer all of the digital agency services ladies and gentlemen don't misconstrue what I'm saying to just be a Facebook ad specialist offer everything but do it just for one niche and watch your revenue and watch your ability to become an expert and get celebrity authority within that niche, watch it become a limitless potential for you and watch how quickly you can expand into that category and more importantly watch how much confidence you get as a digital agency owner from being a specialist within one particular thing. It gives you more confidence, it gives you more ability and more importantly it gives you a potential at least or a probability at the minimum to master what it is you do instead of just being a jack of all trades and a master of none. I encourage you have an expansion and a contraction oriented mindset when looking at your products and services that being said dependent on that second perspective I gave you which I mentioned in my talk at the beginning here, dependent on how well you're selling your products and services and dependent on the attention of where your team is going and how you're flowing the attention of your team to different products and services is going to depend if they have a mindset to expand or if they have a mindset where they're going to contract. What does that mean? Well if you're selling the hell out of a bunch of products and services your team feels that they're crushing it on their ability to deliver you are going to expand. You're going to have the ability to have your team become more confident, more productive, more capabilities will feel like they're in front of themselves and boom. Now all of them can handle three more clients than they thought they could yesterday. That comes from the ability for them to have an expansion oriented mindset. Now they are getting a bunch of clients on their lap that they can't deliver for and you're constantly having to micromanage them and you're batting them down. You just can't get results what's going to happen? They're going to contract. They're going to go inward. They're going to choose to be fearful and every action they take is going to be a reciprocated version of that. They're going to put the energy of themselves into everything that you want them to do for your clients. Why would you not want them to stay where they have that expansion oriented mindset rather than a contraction mindset? You don't want your people to be inward, introverted, small minded, like scary little cowardly people. You want your people to be pounding their chest everywhere they go. Dominate. Ready to crush it for all your clients. You're constantly worried about how they're going to expand into the next set of results that they want. Always, always, always, always, always focused on what the next big thing is. But that comes from you acknowledging results. Perfection means forgiveness and progress means acknowledgement. Always remember that. It's a quote from David Meltzer. When you guys are looking at expansion versus contraction, don't undermine this. This is an incredible lesson under the products and services category.
